label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out why the author wrote this text. Let me start by reading the question again. The options are A through D, and I have to pick the correct one. The question is asking about the purpose of the text. The author's main goal in writing this passage. Let me look back at the text. The author starts by talking about joining an exchange study program and their experience in Paris. They describe meeting host parents, the language barrier, and then a situation where they had to move to a new host family because of a death in the family. Then they meet a Brazilian roommate named Paolo, and they become good friends. The author concludes by recommending an exchange program to others who want to experience foreign cultures and gain friendships. So the text is the author's personal account of their time in an exchange program. They talk about their experiences, the challenges, the people they met, and the friendships formed. The conclusion is a recommendation, but the body of the text is about their personal story. Looking at the options: A) To show the importance of learning foreign cultures. The text does mention experiencing a foreign culture, but the main focus isn't on the importance of learning it. The author's experience is more about their personal journey and friendships. B) To discuss how to build meaningful friendships. The author does talk about making friends with Paolo, but the text isn't a guide or discussion on how to build friendships. It's more of a story about the friendships they formed naturally. C) To explain how exchange study programs work. The author doesn't really explain the mechanics of the program. They mention being chosen, moving to a host family, but it's more about their personal experience rather than explaining the process. D) To describe his experiences in an exchange program. This seems to fit because the entire text is the author recounting their time in the program, the people they met, the challenges, and the friendships
